- Data sources and analogs:

  • CB Insights: US accounts for ~60% of unicorn formations.  
  • Dealroom: US receives ~50–70% of global funding rounds, depending on definition.  
- Key influencing factors:
  • Rapid growth in Asia and Europe may gradually erode US share over time.  
  • Definition of “venture‐backed” (seed through late stage) can shift percentages.  
  • Yearly fluctuations – e.g., pandemic‐driven deal surges in non‐US markets.  
- Limitations and uncertainties:
  • Reporting biases: not all deals are captured equally across regions.  
  • Differences in data coverage between sources.  
  • Evolving ecosystem: emerging markets may increase share in the near future.  
- Subgroups and temporal changes:
  • Industry verticals (biotech vs. software) may be over‐/under‐represented in certain regions.  
  • Trend: US share has declined from ~80% (2000s) to ~60% (recent years).  
- Best guess and uncertainty about the parameter:
  • Best guess (mean): p ≃ 0.60  
  • Standard deviation (σ_p): ≃ 0.07  
- Rationale for σ_p:
  • Captures uncertainty in data reporting, definitional scope, and year‐to‐year variation.  
  • Implies a 95% credible interval roughly [0.60 ± 2·0.07] = [0.46, 0.74], reflecting plausible global shifts and measurement error.  
- Emphasis:
  • σ_p quantifies uncertainty about the true global proportion, not the variability of individual startup locations.  
  • A moderate σ_p acknowledges reasonably strong but imperfect information.
</parameter_estimation_process>

<std_dev>0.07</std_dev>

<justification>
Industry reports (PitchBook, Crunchbase, CB Insights) consistently show the US accounts for about 55–65% of global venture-backed startups. The chosen standard deviation reflects uncertainty due to data coverage differences, definitional boundaries, and evolving market shares.
</justification>

<confidence_level>
Moderately confident. Multiple reputable sources converge near 60%, but reporting biases and dynamic global trends introduce meaningful uncertainty.
</confidence_level>

- Lack of a single global dataset covering all private ventures.
- Survivorship and reporting bias: high-funded companies more visible.
- Changing market conditions—mean may drift year to year.

Subgroup and temporal variation:
- Seed-only portfolio companies average < 5 M; later stages average > 50 M.
- US mean > global mean.
- Funding environment cycles (bull vs bear markets) shift means by ± 5–10 M.

Best guess and uncertainty:
- Best guess (mean) ≈ 20 M USD.
- Standard deviation of our estimate ≈ 5 M USD, reflecting uncertainty about true global mean given skew, data gaps, and temporal changes.

- Above median (~15–20 M) to account for right skew.
- SD of 5 M yields a 95% credible interval (~10–30 M), capturing plausible range.
